baseline_source: Aurore Mk VI WCET Analysis Implementation

executive_summary.key_finding: Mk VI used 10M+ sample statistical WCET analysis with CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, percentiles (P95/P99), and warm-up handling. Mk VII requirements (≤5.0ms WCET, ≤5% jitter at 99.9th percentile) are compatible with Mk VI approach but require increased sample size (100M+) and enhanced telemetry integration.

measurement_approach.timing_header: timing.h provides get_time_raw_ns() and get_time_raw_ms() inline functions

warm_up_handling.rationale: TPU first inference is slower due to delegate initialization

output_format.console_output: Stage-by-stage WCET analysis with formatted tables
output_format.histogram: Not implemented - raw latencies stored in vector

telemetry_integration.rotation_policy: Keep 3 runs, archive older to tar.gz

AM7-L1-SYS-003.description: System timing shall be bounded with: (a) WCET ≤ 5.0ms, (b) jitter ≤ 5% at 99.9th percentile
AM7-L1-SYS-003.measurement_point: interrupt handler entry to GPIO toggle

AM7-L2-TIM-001.description: System shall sustain processing rate of 120 Hz ±1% for minimum 30 minutes

AM7-L2-TIM-002.description: End-to-end latency shall have WCET ≤ 5.0ms measured from interrupt handler entry to GPIO toggle

AM7-L2-TIM-003.description: Runtime jitter shall not exceed 5% of nominal period (417μs at 120Hz) at 99.9th percentile

AM7-L3-TIM-001.description: System shall use CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW (clock_id=4) as sole time source
AM7-L3-TIM-001.verification: binary analysis confirming no gettimeofday(), time(), rdtsc

AM7-L3-TIM-002.verification: static analysis + LD_PRELOAD hooks over 10M cycles

AM7-L3-TIM-003.description: Critical threads shall use SCHED_FIFO: vision_pipeline=90, track_compute=85, actuation_output=95

AM7-L3-TIM-004.description: All loops shall have statically bounded maximum iteration counts

AM7-L3-TIM-005.description: System shall lock all memory pages (mlockall) to prevent page faults

glossary_definitions.WCET: Worst-Case Execution Time determined by measurement-based statistical analysis (100M+ samples under stress)
glossary_definitions.Jitter: Standard deviation of period measurement; specified at 99.9th percentile
glossary_definitions.Deterministic: Timing variation ≤ 5% at 99.9th percentile over 10M consecutive cycles

similarities: Both use CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW as authoritative clock
Both require statistical measurement-based WCET analysis
Both use percentiles (P95, P99) for timing characterization
Both require SCHED_FIFO for real-time threads
Both integrate timing data with telemetry (unified.csv)
differences: Mk VII requires 100M+ samples vs Mk VI 10M+
Mk VII specifies 99.9th percentile jitter vs Mk VI P95/P99
Mk VII WCET budget is 5.0ms vs Mk VI 15-18ms (TPU-focused)
Mk VII requires explicit loop bound verification
Mk VII requires mlockall() memory locking
Mk VII has explicit Ethernet stress test requirement

components.name: StatisticalAnalyzer
components.responsibility: Compute WCET metrics from collected samples

components.name: JitterAnalyzer
components.responsibility: Compute jitter statistics at specified percentiles

sample_size_recommendations.rationale: Per AM7-L2-TIM-002 and AM7-L2-TIM-003, 100M+ samples required for 99.9th percentile statistical confidence
sample_size_recommendations.warmup_rationale: Discard first 1000 samples to account for TPU delegate initialization, cache warm-up, branch predictor training
sample_size_recommendations.parallel_collection: Run multiple instances to reduce collection time

percentiles.p50: Median latency for typical case characterization

test_scenarios.name: baseline_wcet
test_scenarios.description: WCET measurement with no operator load

test_scenarios.name: ethernet_stress_wcet
test_scenarios.description: WCET under Ethernet saturation (iperf3 900Mbps + ping flood)

test_scenarios.name: jitter_analysis
test_scenarios.description: Period jitter measurement over 10M consecutive cycles

new_functions.name: wcet_start_collection()
new_functions.description: Initialize WCET collection with target sample count

new_functions.name: wcet_record_sample()
new_functions.description: Record a single latency sample in nanoseconds

new_functions.name: wcet_compute_statistics()
new_functions.description: Compute all WCET statistics from collected samples

new_functions.name: wcet_export_csv()
new_functions.description: Export raw samples to CSV for external analysis

functionality.name: preflight_check
commands: check disk space for sample storage (≥10GB)

functionality.name: ethernet_stress_collection
commands: stop iperf3 and ping after collection

functionality.name: statistical_analysis
commands: compute percentiles (P50, P95, P99, P99.9, P99.99)

optimization_recommendations.description: Implement lock-free ring buffer for WCET sample collection
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Prevents collection overhead from affecting measured latencies

optimization_recommendations.description: Pre-allocate all sample storage before collection starts
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Prevents allocation latency spikes during measurement

optimization_recommendations.description: Use ARM NEON SIMD for statistical computations
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Accelerates percentile calculations on large datasets

optimization_recommendations.description: Implement parallel collection across multiple CPU cores
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Reduces total collection time from hours to minutes

optimization_recommendations.description: Add real-time telemetry streaming during collection
optimization_recommendations.rationale: Allows monitoring collection progress without affecting results

baseline_rationale: Mk VI WCET methodology is compatible with Mk VII requirements. Mk VI used CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, statistical analysis with percentiles, warm-up handling, and telemetry integration. Mk VII requires 10x more samples (100M vs 10M), explicit 99.9th percentile jitter measurement, and Ethernet stress testing. All Mk VII timing requirements can be verified using an enhanced version of Mk VI's approach.
